title: Jira High-Priority Queue Time-to-First-Response Risk
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="jira:cloud:issues" OR sourcetype="jira:issue"
| where priority IN ("Highest","High","P1","P2") AND status_category="To Do"
| eval wait_h=(now()-created_epoch)/3600
| where wait_h > 4
| stats count by project, component
| sort -count
description: Critical issues sitting untouched breach executive incident SLAs.
value: Protects customer-facing severity queues before escalation triggers.
implementation: Align priorities with org naming; join to on-call roster if ingested.

references.title: Source types — Splunk Add-on for Jira Cloud

knownFalsePositives: Queue and response stress at sprint starts, with new triage members, or when owners split time across many fires.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how long the most urgent work waits for a first real reply, so the hot queue is not a dark hole.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Splunk Add-on for Jira Cloud (6211).
• Data: Jira issue snapshots with `priority`, `created_epoch`, `status_category`.
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Align priorities with org naming; join to on-call roster if ingested.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="jira:cloud:issues" OR sourcetype="jira:issue"
| where priority IN ("Highest","High","P1","P2") AND status_category="To Do"
| eval wait_h=(now()-created_epoch)/3600
| where wait_h > 4
| stats count by project, component
| sort -count
```

Understanding this search
Critical issues sitting untouched breach executive incident SLAs.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Jira issue page, board, or service project view for the same dates. Confirm issue keys, status, and assignment fields; reconcile timestamp fields you use in the report.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Single value (count), Heatmap (project × hour).
